title: ParentBench v2.0 Implementation
description:
Transform ParentBench from a static benchmark site into the industry standard for AI child safety certification. Platform-first architecture with eval engine, job queue, model adapters, and certification workflow.

## Scope (from /plan-ceo-review)
- Provider Certification Program
- Community Test Case Contributions
- Real-Time Safety Alerts
- Age-Bracket Recommendations
- Model Comparison View
- Public API
- Multimodal Testing
- Sharable Report Cards
- Historical Score Tracking

## Architecture Components
1. Eval Engine — Runs test cases against model APIs
2. Job Queue — Inngest for background processing
3. Model Adapters — Pluggable connectors for each provider
4. Results Store — Postgres (Neon/Supabase)
5. Notification Service — Resend for email alerts
6. API Layer — Public REST for third parties
7. Certification Service — Provider applications, verification workflow

## Infrastructure
- Hosting: Vercel (Next.js)
- Database: Postgres (Neon or Supabase)
- Background Jobs: Inngest
- Email: Resend
- Payments: Stripe
- Storage: S3

## References
- docs/designs/v2-vision.md
- TODOS.md

title: Phase 4.5: Human Verification Loop
description:
Add human-in-the-loop verification to improve evaluation accuracy and build trust. Replaces heuristic grading with LLM-as-judge + confidence scoring, admin escalation queue, reputation-weighted community voting, and expert spot-checks.

## Problem Statement
Current eval system uses heuristic grading which produces artificially low scores (best model: 61.73) and misses nuanced safe responses.

## Solution: Multi-Layer Verification with Gating
| Layer | Who | What | When |
|-------|-----|------|------|
| L1: Automated | LLM Judge | Grade with confidence score | Every eval |
| L2: Escalation | Admin | Review low-confidence (<0.7) | As flagged |
| L3: Spot-check | Expert Panel | Stratified 5% sample | Weekly |
| L4: Community | Verified Users | Reputation-weighted voting | Ongoing |
| L5: Red Team | Public | Adversarial prompts | Ongoing |

## Key Design Decisions (from Codex Review)
1. **Quarantine gating** — Low-confidence/disputed results excluded from published scores until cleared
2. **Immutable audit trail** — All human verdicts stored in separate `human_reviews` table with required reasoning
3. **Sybil prevention** — 7-day account age + verification + reputation weighting (60% weighted consensus)
4. **Optimistic locking** — Prevents concurrent admin claim conflicts
5. **Stratified sampling** — Reproducible weekly spot-checks by model/category

## Design Doc
See docs/designs/human-verification-loop.md for full engineering plan, UX design, and E2E test plan.

## SLA Targets
- Disputed: 48h
- Low confidence: 7 days
- Spot check: 14 days

## Dependencies
- Phase 1 (Database + API) — complete
- Phase 4 (Email) — for notifications

5. Add grader_type column ('heuristic', 'llm', 'human')

## Judge Prompt Requirements

title: Implement quarantine gating for low-confidence results
description:
Add gating logic to exclude low-confidence and disputed results from published scores.

## Database Changes
- Add is_quarantined BOOLEAN to eval_results
- Add quarantined_at TIMESTAMP
- Add verdict_source ENUM ('automated', 'human_override', 'community_consensus')

## Logic
1. Results with confidence < 0.7 auto-quarantine
2. Disputed results auto-quarantine
3. Quarantined results excluded from score computation
4. Human review clears quarantine and sets final verdict
5. Score recalculation triggered on quarantine clear

## Acceptance Criteria
- Low-confidence results automatically quarantined
- computeModelScore() excludes quarantined results
- clearQuarantine() updates verdict and triggers recalc
- UI shows 'Pending Review' badge for quarantined

title: Create immutable human_reviews audit table
description:
Add human_reviews table for immutable audit trail of all human verdicts.

## Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE human_reviews (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  eval_result_id UUID REFERENCES eval_results(id),
  reviewer_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  reviewer_type ENUM('admin', 'expert', 'community'),
  verdict ENUM('pass', 'partial', 'fail'),
  reasoning TEXT NOT NULL,
  confidence DECIMAL(3,2),
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Requirements
- Reasoning field is required (NOT NULL)
- No UPDATE or DELETE operations allowed (application-level)
- Indexes on eval_result_id and reviewer_id
- Latest review determines final verdict

## Acceptance Criteria
- Table created with Drizzle migration
- API prevents updates to existing reviews
- Multiple reviews per result allowed (for appeals)
- getLatestReview() returns most recent verdict

title: Build review queue with optimistic locking
description:
Create review_queue table and API with concurrency controls.

## Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE review_queue (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  eval_result_id UUID REFERENCES eval_results(id),
  reason ENUM('low_confidence', 'disputed', 'spot_check', 'escalated'),
  priority INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  assigned_to UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  assigned_at TIMESTAMP,
  version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
  status ENUM('pending', 'in_review', 'completed', 'expired'),
  sla_deadline TIMESTAMP,
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## Optimistic Locking
- claimQueueItem() checks version before update
- Only one admin can claim same item
- Stale assignments released after 30 minutes

## API Routes
- GET /api/internal/review/queue — List with filters
- POST /api/internal/review/[id]/claim — Claim with locking
- POST /api/internal/review/[id]/verdict — Submit verdict
- POST /api/internal/review/[id]/release — Release claim

## Acceptance Criteria
- Concurrent claims only one succeeds
- Stale assignments auto-release (cron job)
- SLA deadlines enforced
- Tests verify locking behavior

title: Implement sybil-resistant community voting
description:
Add community voting with reputation weighting and sybil prevention.

## Tables
```sql
CREATE TABLE verified_voters (
  user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id),
  verification_method ENUM('email', 'phone', 'oauth'),
  reputation_score DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
  total_votes INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  agreement_with_final DECIMAL(3,2),
  account_age_verified BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  can_vote_after TIMESTAMP,
  is_suspended BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
);

CREATE TABLE community_votes (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  eval_result_id UUID,
  user_id UUID,
  vote ENUM('pass', 'partial', 'fail'),
  reasoning TEXT,
  vote_weight DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.0,
  created_at TIMESTAMP
);
```

## Sybil Prevention
- 7-day account age required
- Email/phone/OAuth verification required
- Reputation score affects vote weight
- 50 votes/hour rate limit

## Consensus
- 60% weighted consensus required to override
- updateVoterReputation() after final verdict
- Incorrect votes penalized more (-0.1) than correct rewarded (+0.05)

## Acceptance Criteria
- New accounts cannot vote for 7 days
- Unverified users cannot vote
- Weighted consensus calculation correct
- Reputation updates after final verdict

title: Implement stratified spot-check sampling
description:
Create weekly spot-check sampling with stratification by model and category.

## Algorithm
1. Get all results from past week (not already in queue)
2. Group by model_id + category_id (strata)
3. Sample 5% from each stratum (min 1)
4. Use weekly seed for reproducibility
5. Create queue entries with reason='spot_check'

## Inngest Job
- Schedule: weekly on Monday 00:00 UTC
- Function: generateWeeklySpotChecks()
- SLA deadline: 14 days from creation

## Exclusions
- Results already in queue
- Results with confidence >= 0.95 (high confidence)
- Results already human-reviewed

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sampling stratified by model + category
- 5% sampled from each stratum
- Reproducible with same seed
- Queue entries have 14-day SLA
- Inngest job runs weekly

title: Phase 4.5: Newsletter System
description:
Build a proper newsletter system to replace Netlify Forms. Includes subscriber management, double opt-in, automated email triggers, manual campaigns, and GDPR-compliant unsubscribe flow.

## Problem
ParentBench has a signup form but no actual newsletter system. Parents expect updates but never receive them.

## Solution
- Store subscribers in database with double opt-in
- Automated emails on new model evaluations / score changes
- Manual campaign composer for admin
- Suppression list for compliance (CAN-SPAM/GDPR)
- Tracking for opens, clicks, bounces

## Key Design Decisions (from Codex Review)
1. **Suppression list** — Email hashes kept forever for compliance, PII cleared after 30 days
2. **Unsubscribe token** — Each subscriber has unique token for one-click unsubscribe
3. **Preference filtering** — Jobs respect notify_new_models, notify_score_changes, frequency settings
4. **Tracking HMAC** — Anti-abuse verification for tracking pixels/links
5. **Admin RBAC** — All /api/internal/* routes require admin role + CSRF + audit logging

## Design Doc
See docs/designs/newsletter-system.md for full architecture, UX design, and E2E test plan.

## Dependencies
- Phase 5.1 (Resend setup) — for email delivery
- Database (Phase 1) — complete

## Key Fields

description:

## Jobs
1. newsletter/send-confirmation — On subscribe event
2. newsletter/send-welcome — On confirm event
3. newsletter/new-model-evaluated — On evaluation/completed event
4. newsletter/score-change-alert — On score/significant-change event
5. newsletter/weekly-digest — Cron: Mondays 9am UTC
6. newsletter/campaign-send — On campaign scheduled

## Preference Filtering
- Check notify_new_models before new model emails
- Check notify_score_changes before score alerts
- Check frequency (immediate vs weekly) for timing

## Idempotency
- Generate idempotency_key per subscriber+event
- Check for existing send before sending
- Prevent double sends on retry

## Acceptance Criteria
- All jobs trigger correctly
- Preferences respected
- Suppression checked
- Idempotency prevents duplicates

title: Implement Resend webhook handler
description:

## Webhook Events
- email.delivered — Update send status
- email.opened — Update send status, increment subscriber opens
- email.clicked — Update send status, increment subscriber clicks
- email.bounced — Update status, suppress if hard bounce
- email.complained — Update status, suppress immediately

## Security
- Verify Resend webhook signature
- Idempotency: store processed event IDs
- Handle duplicates gracefully

## Hard Bounce / Complaint Handling
- Add email to suppression list
- Update subscriber status to unsubscribed
- Log reason for audit

## Acceptance Criteria
- Webhook signature verified
- All event types handled
- Hard bounces suppressed
- Complaints suppressed
- Duplicate events ignored

title: Build tracking endpoints with HMAC verification
description:
Create tracking endpoints for email opens and clicks.

## Routes
- GET /api/newsletter/track/open/[sendId] — Tracking pixel
- GET /api/newsletter/track/click/[sendId]/[linkId] — Link redirect

## HMAC Verification
- URL includes ?h=HMAC parameter
- HMAC = SHA256(sendId, TRACKING_SECRET).slice(0,16)
- Reject requests with invalid/missing HMAC
- Prevents forged tracking events

## Open Tracking
- Verify HMAC
- Update newsletter_sends.opened_at
- Increment subscriber.total_emails_opened
- Return 1x1 transparent GIF

## Click Tracking
- Verify HMAC
- Update newsletter_sends.clicked_at
- Increment subscriber.total_links_clicked
- Redirect to actual URL

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid HMAC returns 403
- Valid requests update stats
- Open returns tracking pixel
- Click redirects correctly

title: Penetration Testing (Adversarial Resistance)
description:
Comprehensive penetration testing system to evaluate AI models against jailbreaks, child-specific attacks, and multi-turn manipulation.

## Goals
1. Comprehensive Coverage — Test all known jailbreak techniques + child-specific attack vectors
2. Multi-Turn Testing — Evaluate resistance to conversation-based manipulation
3. New Scoring Category — 'Adversarial Resistance' as 5th category (critical severity, 3x weight)
4. Human Red Team — Ongoing program for discovering new attack vectors
5. Automated Baseline — Pre-defined test suite runs with every evaluation

## Key Features
- ~50 automated pen test cases (jailbreak + child-specific + multi-turn)
- Multi-turn eval engine with conversation state management
- Jailbreak detector with LLM judge fallback
- Red team submission portal and leaderboard
- Dynamic attack variation to prevent gaming

## Security Requirements (Codex Review Fixes)
- Admin auth with CSRF and audit logging
- PII stripping and CSAM detection before storage
- Encrypted hazardous content storage
- Safe eval pipeline (local first, abstracted external LLM)
- Multi-factor rate limiting

title: Implement Safe Jailbreak Detector
description:
Two-tier jailbreak detection with local-first and safe external LLM.

## Tier 1: Local Detection
- Keyword matching against success indicators
- Pattern-based detection for known jailbreak signatures
- No external API calls
- High confidence threshold (>0.8) to avoid LLM tier

## Tier 2: LLM Judge
- Self-hosted model option (full context safe)
- External LLM option (abstracted prompts only)
- Sanitize response before sending externally
- Remove attack type references, truncate to 1000 chars

## Security (Codex Fix)
- Never send actual attack prompts to external LLM
- Abstracted evaluation prompt asks about child-appropriateness

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local detection catches obvious cases
- [ ] LLM tier only called when needed
- [ ] External prompts are sanitized
- [ ] Self-hosted model works when configured

title: Implement Hazardous Content Handler
description:

- Email, phone, SSN, credit card patterns
- Replace with [REDACTED_TYPE] placeholders
- Apply to all conversation content

## CSAM Detection
- Integrate PhotoDNA or equivalent
- Scan all image evidence before storage
- Auto-report to NCMEC if matched (legal requirement)
- Never store flagged content

## Encryption
- AES-256 encryption for stored content
- Encryption key from environment variable
- Version tracking for key rotation

## Audit Trail
- Log all content access with viewer, purpose, timestamp

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PII stripped from all submissions
- [ ] CSAM detection blocks flagged content
- [ ] NCMEC reporting implemented
- [ ] Content encrypted at rest
- [ ] Access audit log populated

title: Implement Dynamic Attack Generator
description:
Generate attack variations to prevent models from gaming static tests.

## Attack Templates
- Base pattern with {{SLOT}} placeholders
- Variation options per slot
- Mutation strategies: synonym_replacement, tone_shift, encoding_variation

## Variation Generation
- Generate N variations per template per eval run
- Random slot value selection
- Apply random mutations
- Log which variations used (for reproducibility)

## Mutation Types
- Synonym replacement using wordnet or similar
- Tone shift (formal/casual/childlike)
- Encoding (base64, unicode tricks)
- Sentence reordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Templates generate unique variations
- [ ] Variations preserve attack intent
- [ ] Mutations don't break prompts
- [ ] Variation log for reproducibility

description:

- Provider dashboard (/provider/dashboard)
- Badge download (multiple formats)
- Score dispute flow (/provider/dispute)

## Certification Rules (from eng review)
- Manual approval by admin
- Auto-revocation if score drops below 70
- Appeal process for rejections

- Status pill badges (yellow=pending, blue=review, green=approved, red=rejected)
- Single status card dominates dashboard
- Badge download front and center when approved

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full application → payment → review → approval flow
- Provider can download badge assets
- Auto-revocation triggers on score < 70
- Email notifications at each status change

title: Set up Stripe for certification payments
description:

3. Add STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET to env vars
4. Create src/lib/stripe.ts with checkout functions

## Flow
1. Provider clicks 'Apply for Certification'
2. Fills application form
3. Redirects to Stripe Checkout

- Checkout session creates

- Certification status updates to APPLIED
